# Message Decision Packs - Full Agent Context

Last updated: 2026-07-21

Message Decision Packs (MDP) is a local/offline file standard, Rust CLI, and agent plugin for making decision context explicit and testable.

The core rule is simple:

```text
Do not ask an agent to remember the messaging or review system.
Put the judgment in local files, validate it, route only the needed entries, and check the output.
```

## Product Boundary

MDP stores source evidence, fit or review rules, approved claims or proof, routing contracts, output boundaries, gaps, and evals. It can validate packs, route entries, evaluate fit/readiness, produce bounded briefs, check claims, verify proof bindings, render review artifacts, and surface gaps.

MDP is not:

- an AI SDR, CRM, sequencer, enrichment provider, scraper, or BI tool;
- a proposal management system or approval workflow owner;
- a generic automation platform.

Sending, CRM mutation, enrichment, scraping, sequencing, proposal submission, legal/compliance approval, and external automation remain outside MDP.

## Repository Shape

```text
message-decision-packs/
  cli/      # Rust `mdp` CLI
  plugin/   # Pluxx plugin package source: skills, assets, hooks, scripts
  docs/     # current user and maintainer docs
  examples/ # one canonical runnable Eve example
```

CLI behavior, schema contracts, starter templates, docs, and skills must stay aligned.

## Install And Update

```bash
bash <(curl -fsSL https://mdp.orchidlabs.dev/install.sh) --agents -y
```

Use `--cli` for the CLI only or `--codex`, `--claude-code`, `--cursor`, or `--opencode` for one host bundle. The installer uses GitHub release assets and pairs agent instructions with the matching CLI. Rerun it to update or set `MDP_VERSION` to pin a release.

Verify:

```bash
mdp --version
mdp --json doctor --dir .
mdp --json capabilities
```

`mdp --json capabilities` is the canonical machine-readable command, side-effect, strict-mode, dry-run, and error-contract inventory.

## Pack Layout

```text
.mdp/
  manifest.yaml
  sources.yaml
  source-strategy.json   # optional reviewed discovery plan
  prompts/*.yaml         # optional normalization/extraction contracts
  cards/*.yaml
  briefs/
  evals/*.yaml
```

The manifest declares pack identity, profiles, input contracts, card paths, jobs, routing, and skill surface. The source ledger distinguishes direct facts from interpretations and gaps. Cards hold modular decisions. Evals protect route, fit, brief, prompt-output, proof-output, claim, and rendering behavior.

## Universal Primitive Model

Every profile maps domain vocabulary onto ten primitives:

| Primitive | GTM expression | Proposal expression |
|---|---|---|
| `actors` | personas | proposal roles |
| `decision-criteria` | fit/disqualification rules | bid/no-bid and evaluation criteria |
| `source-signals` | account/person signals | opportunity and requirement signals |
| `needs-requirements` | pains and readiness needs | requirements matrix |
| `evidence-proof` | positioning and approved claims | proof library and past performance |
| `boundaries` | avoid-rules and objections | compliance and proposal boundaries |
| `output-contracts` | output rules, hooks, CTAs, patterns | review outputs and response rules |
| `routing-jobs` | motions and channel policies | review gates and jobs |
| `gaps` | missing evidence or owner context | unsupported requirements or proof |
| `evals` | route, fit, brief, and copy checks | review, proof, and safety checks |

Account context, opportunity context, personas, proposal roles, and other domain terms belong to profile-owned input contracts and card IDs. They are not separate core engines.

## GTM Decision Flow

```text
supplied source row
  -> optional normalize-prospect prompt contract
  -> provider-neutral prospect JSON
  -> deterministic fit gate
  -> routed context entries
  -> bounded brief
  -> agent draft, only when allowed
  -> claim/output checks
```

Use `company_domain` as the stronger account key for new workflows while keeping `company` as the human-readable name. MDP canonicalizes supplied domains locally; it does not browse, DNS-check, enrich, or infer a domain from a company name.

Packs can declare required fields, signals, attributes, value contracts, and attribute definitions. Keep bounded reviewed metadata in `attributes` and evidence/provenance in `signals[].source`.

The fit gate returns `fit`, `disqualified`, or `insufficient-context`. Packs can declare `qualification_gates` in `manifest.yaml` for requirements such as public-person resolution and minimum or maximum source-backed signal coverage across fit and why-now categories. The CLI owns those gates; external scouts gather and normalize evidence but must not recreate the pass/fail policy. Stop on `disqualified` or `insufficient-context` unless the user explicitly requests a different analysis. Do not invent missing person, account, proof, or timing facts to unlock drafting.

## Portfolio Scope And Routed Output Constraints

A GTM profile can declare context dimensions such as `product`, `capability`, `solution`, or `segment`. Card entries use `scope` to narrow where an existing universal-primitive decision applies; dimensions do not create new primitives or separate engines. Unscoped entries remain global.

Use repeatable `--scope dimension=value` selectors on `route` and route-scoped `check-claims`. Prospect-driven `fit` and `brief` derive declared scope from scalar prospect attributes, with top-level `segment` used for a declared segment dimension. Missing or invalid scope blocks portfolio-sensitive drafting instead of mixing entries across products.

`check-claims` applies global output rules plus the rules selected by persona, job, and scope. Supported deterministic checks include unsupported claims, avoid literals, word and question limits, subject constraints, forbidden links/HTML/tracking, and routed `exact_paragraphs`. Target ranges are advisory unless strict mode promotes them; hard bounds fail the gate.

## Proposal Review Flow

Proposal profiles use the same primitives without manufacturing prospect rows:

```text
approved opportunity, requirement, and proof sources
  -> source ledger and optional source audit
  -> fresh/stateless normalization with declared inputs only
  -> validate prompt output and create run receipt
  -> proposal roles, criteria, requirements, proof, and boundaries
  -> validate
  -> route the review job
  -> verify source-bound generated claims
  -> surface gaps and human sign-off needs
```

Public artifacts must remain generic, synthetic, or explicitly sanitized. Never invent certifications, compliance status, past performance, or proof. MDP supports review and gap detection; it does not replace compliance, legal, procurement, proposal management, or approval ownership. Same-conversation normalization is advisory unless `mdp run-receipt` records an isolated, declared-input-only run.

## CLI Contract

```bash
mdp --json doctor --dir .
mdp --json validate --dir .
mdp --json skills --dir .
mdp --json skills --dir . --job prospect-fit-or-brief
mdp --json --summary route --entries --dir . --persona "PMM" --job "linkedin outbound copy"
mdp --json route --entries --dir . --persona "PMM" --job "portfolio scope example" --scope product=local-cli
mdp --json fit --dir . --prospect <prospect.json>
mdp --json --summary brief --context --dir . --prospect <prospect.json> --channel linkedin
mdp render-brief --dir . --file <brief.json> --template gtm-prospect
mdp --json check-claims --dir . --text "<draft copy>" --persona "PMM" --job "linkedin outbound copy"
mdp --json check-claims --dir . --text "<draft copy>" --persona "PMM" --job "portfolio scope example" --scope product=local-cli
mdp --json author-proof-output --dir . --draft <proof-output-draft.json> --out <proof-output.json>
mdp --json verify-output --dir . --file <proof-output.json>
mdp --json run-receipt --dir . --workflow proposal-review --isolation isolated --declared-inputs-only --prompt-id normalize-opportunity --prompt-output <prompt-output.json> --validation <validation-result.json> --source-audit <source-audit.json>
mdp --json gaps --dir .
mdp --json eval --dir .
```

Use `brief --context` and draft from `data.context.entries`. Open `data.context.full_card_required` paths only when present. Human-readable renderers are review layers, not machine sources of truth or permission to use blocked output.

`check-claims` handles plain text against claims, avoid-rules, output-rules, and constraints. When generated output cites source, card, proof, requirement, or template IDs, require a `mdp.proof-output.v0` artifact and run `verify-output`; model-written IDs alone are not proof. Use `author-proof-output` to compile `mdp.proof-output-draft.v0` segments into that artifact, but do not treat it as a source audit or verifier bypass.

Generated starter rows carry `source_kind: synthetic-example` and `synthetic: true`. Rows created by `sample-leads` additionally carry `do_not_contact: true`. Never enrich, upload, sequence, contact, or present either kind as a real prospect.

## Prompt And Source-Strategy Contracts

Prompt files use `format: mdp.prompt.v0` and return `contract: mdp.prompt-output.v0`:

- normalization prompts turn messy supplied rows into validated prospect or opportunity context;
- extraction prompts propose reviewable card patches, gaps, rejected claims, confidence, and provenance.

`source_summary.inputs_used` names exact declared prompt inputs only, such as `raw_row`, `raw_opportunity`, `existing_pack_context`, or `source_kind`. Field paths, snippets, URLs, PDF/page locators, and review notes belong in source/provenance locators such as `signals[].source`, entry `evidence`/`provenance`, and normalization trace. Proposal normalization keeps `normalized_prospect` for compatibility and may include `normalized_opportunity` only as an exact alias.

Prompt contracts do not browse or execute. Source-strategy artifacts plan approved source targets, queries, evidence rules, exclusions, receipts, budgets, review gates, and downstream handoff instructions. External scouts may execute an approved strategy, but MDP remains the local scoring and routing authority.

## Plugin And Pluxx

The full repository is the product/plugin source contract: CLI, docs, canonical templates/assets, authored skills under `plugin/skills`, install/release assets, and Pluxx config move together. Pluxx packages canonical source into release bundles for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and OpenCode. The MDP installer combines those bundles with the Rust CLI; Pluxx is not the MDP runtime or a hosted service.

MDP ships exactly five skills:

- `mdp`: CLI/operator help and mixed MDP coordination;
- `mdp-pack-builder`: source-grounded GTM or proposal pack authoring;
- `mdp-pack-review`: structural, routing/eval, and installed-artifact QA;
- `mdp-gtm-brief`: GTM fit, pre-draft copy context, and supplied-copy review;
- `mdp-proposal-review`: bounded bid/no-bid, compliance, proof, and red-team review.

Use `mdp --json skills --dir <pack> --job <job-id>` before profile-sensitive work. `packaged_skill_ids` is the released inventory; `eligibility` is pack policy; `host_discovery.status: unobserved` means MDP does not claim to hide or expose skills in the agent host. Unknown and profile-crossing jobs have no fallback.

## Canonical Runnable Example

[AI SDR Eve on Vercel](https://github.com/orchidautomation/message-decision-packs/tree/main/examples/ai-sdr-eve-vercel) is the sole canonical runnable example. Eve is the runtime and MDP is its decision/context source of truth.

The example:

- loads a synthetic MDP pack and reviewed source strategy;
- gathers bounded public-source evidence through approved provider tools;
- targets three qualified people per live run, continuing across approved strategy prompts until the target or bounded exhaustion;
- uses pack-owned `qualification_gates` to require person-level public receipts and source-backed fit/why-now evidence;
- runs MDP validation, fit, brief, and claim gates;
- appends reviewed ledger rows;
- does not send outreach or sync a CRM.

## Agent Operating Rules

- Load the manifest first.
- Preserve source provenance and explicit gaps.
- Validate before relying on a pack.
- Normalize supplied inputs before deterministic gates.
- Use routed entries instead of reading every card.
- Stop on no-draft, blocked review, missing proof, or insufficient context.
- Never turn a synthetic fixture into a real prospect or claim.
- Run deterministic checks before approval.
- Re-run evals when pack behavior changes.
- Keep external execution outside MDP and require explicit user approval for it.

Released agent context is available at <https://mdp.orchidlabs.dev/llms.txt> and <https://mdp.orchidlabs.dev/llms-full.txt>.

## Validation

```bash
cargo test --manifest-path cli/Cargo.toml
cargo run --manifest-path cli/Cargo.toml -- --json validate --dir plugin/assets/templates/basic
make validate
```

Run the documented installer and smoke-test the installed artifact after release-affecting changes.
